name: list_work_items_by_query
description: Run a Work Item Query Language (WIQL) query to find work items matching specific criteria. WIQL syntax is similar to SQL: SELECT fields FROM workitems WHERE conditions. Supports filtering by state, assigned user, type, area path, iteration path, tags, and more. Returns work item IDs which can then be fetched for full details.

parameters.name: organization
parameters.description: The name of the Azure DevOps organization.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The name or ID of the project.

parameters.name: api-version
parameters.description: Version of the API to use. Use '7.1'.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The WIQL query string. Example: SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title] FROM workitems WHERE [System.State] = 'Active' AND [System.AssignedTo] = @Me ORDER BY [System.ChangedDate] DESC

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default is 200.

body.query: SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.State], [System.AssignedTo] FROM workitems WHERE [System.TeamProject] = 'MyWebApp' AND [System.State] = 'Active' AND [System.WorkItemType] = 'Bug' ORDER BY [Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority] ASC
